# Questions in Livestream 99

Live Stream #99: 9000 at 99

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=341" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:41</a> | What books belong to the Old Testament, and does including or leaving out the Apocrypha have doctrinal consequences? | He accepts the Hebrew canon, argues the Apocrypha has historical and theological problems, and says later canon adoption was tied to church-history and doctrinal disputes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=2432" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:32</a> | Does Second Temple adultery law explain the lack of a male adulterer in John 7:53-8:11, and is the passage authentic? | The legal background helps explain the scene, but he treats the passage itself as textually doubtful in John while possibly preserving an early Jesus tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=2661" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:21</a> | Did he watch Jeff Durbin and James White's exchange with Brandon Robertson, and what are his thoughts? | He had not seen that exchange, so he comments only generally that he dislikes tag-team debates and presuppositional apologetics. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=3024" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:24</a> | What about theistic evolution as defended by John Walton and Andrew Loke? | He says theistic evolution has problems because it tries to force modern evolutionary theory into Genesis 1. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=3327" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:27</a> | Does `according to their kinds` in Genesis rule out theistic evolution? | Not necessarily. He said it mainly rules out extreme `hopeful monster` style transformation, not every evolutionary model. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=3568" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:28</a> | What is the genre of the book of Jonah? | He says it uses multiple genres, including narrative, historical material, poetry, and prophetic literature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=4032" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:12</a> | Did Michael Heiser argue there was no rebellion of angels before the fall, and does he agree? | He broadly agrees with Heiser's sequence of three rebellions: humanity, angels, and Babel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=4356" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:36</a> | Why were David's sons allowed to be priests in 2 Samuel 8:18? | He says `priests` there means royal or government ministers, not priests of the Lord in the temple. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=5093" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:53</a> | Could Abraham have traveled from Ur to Canaan on the Mari or Old Babylonian trade route? | Yes. He says that route crossed northern Mesopotamia and the Fertile Crescent, so Abraham could have used it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=5168" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:08</a> | What is a steel-man case against the late Exodus? | The best attack would challenge whether the group at Avaris actually left Egypt rather than merely relocating within Egypt, though he does not find it compelling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=5479" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:19</a> | What are his thoughts on C. John Collins's work on Genesis? | He has not read Collins's Genesis work, though he knows Collins's apocalyptic-literature scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=5578" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:58</a> | Are there pictures of aliens in hieroglyphics, and how could pyramids be built without aliens? | No. He points to Egyptian building traditions, canals, boats, ramps, pulleys, worker villages, and construction evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=6119" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:59</a> | Was archaic language used in antiquity to give literature a foreign flavor, and could that explain Egyptian loanwords in Exodus? | He says archaizing usually signaled prestige or classic style, not foreignness, so it does not really explain the Exodus loanwords. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=6414" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:54</a> | Do current seminaries teach about engaging the spirit world, magic, occult topics, or vampires? | Very rarely. He knew of one seminary course, but says schools tend to avoid such subjects despite their pastoral usefulness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=7061" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:41</a> | What does he mean by `strange` paranormal stories from seminary? | He means reports such as electrical interference, lights flickering, flying objects, poltergeist-like activity, and seeing spirits. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=7184" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:44</a> | Is Ezekiel 26 a failed prophecy against Tyre? | He argued no, reading it as a many-nations judgment in which Nebuchadnezzar is only the first major agent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=7765" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:25</a> | What is the earliest New Testament manuscript found outside Egypt, and why are the oldest ones from Egypt? | He says non-Egyptian finds are later, often Antioch-area and mid-third-century; Egypt preserves older manuscripts because of dry conditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=7995" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:15</a> | Is there scholarly substance behind an early date for Revelation before Jerusalem's fall in AD 70? | Not much, in his view. He favors a late first-century date and finds preterist arguments unpersuasive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=8345" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:05</a> | What about sin offerings during the millennium in Ezekiel 40-48? | He gives only a general answer: sacrifices continue, sin offerings did not themselves forgive sin, and a memorial or thanksgiving role is possible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=8894" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:14</a> | How does he reconcile his view on Urkesh with Acts 7:4? | He says `land of the Chaldeans` may reflect a textual corruption or later development that became historically apt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=9437" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:17</a> | Do Genesis 1 parallels in other cultures imply oral descent from Adam, and is that only realistic with young-earth creationism? | Oral descent is possible, but he says it is not limited to young-earth creationism and he avoids firm compatibilist models. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=9600" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:00</a> | What does he think about the Discovery Channel and History Channel, especially as sources for aliens-and-hieroglyphics claims? | He says they are entertainment, not reliable history, and specifically rejects `ancient aliens` style readings of hieroglyphs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=9997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:37</a> | What is his opinion on Gobekli Tepe and its relevance for a model of history? | It is a very old ritual site tied to early agriculture in Anatolia; he says excavation is ongoing and historically important. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=10997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:17</a> | Is Anubis a bad god, and did he have a brother named Beerus? | No Beerus. Anubis was neither simply good nor bad, and Egyptian gods could have both benevolent and dangerous aspects. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=11307" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:27</a> | Did Old Testament offerings have any Egyptian parallels? | Some shared reciprocal-economy logic exists, but Egyptian offerings mainly fed gods and the dead while Israelite offerings had different ritual categories and purposes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=11820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:00</a> | If overindulgence is not gluttony, is it still a sin? | Not necessarily. He defines biblical gluttony as taking more than one's share so others suffer, while noting overindulgence can still become sinful in some circumstances. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=12328" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:28</a> | Who is his favorite Christian philosopher historically, and why? | Soren Kierkegaard, especially for devotional Christian reflection rather than existentialism generally. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/X1Q7ZZ5Q6vg?t=12546" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:06</a> | Should historians be able to investigate miracles as historians, and why or why not? | Historians can study claims, witnesses, and surrounding evidence, but proving the miracle itself usually exceeds historical method. |
